zh.lifeSummary: Guillaume de Sainte Maure et de Précigny出生在法國。他於1205年之前與Avoye de Sainte Maure結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1209年之後。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Guillaume de Sainte Maure et de Précigny出生在法国。他于1205年之前与Avoye de Sainte Maure结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1209年之后。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Sainte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Sain   1, a surname of German origin.
French: probably a variant of Saïn (see Sain 6).
West African (Gambia): variant of the Serer name Sène (see Sene 2).

variants.name: Sain
meanings: Americanized form of German Zirn, which is a variant of Zürn (see Zurn ). Compare Saine , Sane , and Sayne .

Croatian and Serbian (Šain): metonymic occupational name for a hawker or a nickname from ša(h)in ‘falcon, hawk (trained for hunting)’, a word of Turkish and ultimately Persian origin (see Sahin ). Compare Shain and Shine .

Americanized form of Slovenian and Croatian Šajn: nickname from šajn, an obsolete loanword from German meaning ‘shine’, or a Slavicized form of German Schein . As a Croatian surname it may also be a variant of Šain (see 2 above). Compare Shain and Shine .

variants.name: Sene
meanings: French (Sené and Séné): nickname from Old French sené ‘wise, sensible’.

West African (Senegal; Sène): name found among the Serer people, of unexplained etymology. The same name forms part of the name of Roog Seen, the supreme deity in the indigenous Serer religion. Compare Saine 3.

nameMeanings.name: Maure
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Ernesto, Ramon, Adela, Carlos, Eladio, Emilia, Estrella, Horacio, Jorge, Jose, Juan, Mario.  Italian Alessandra, Angelo, Cecilio, Cosimo, Gianfranco, Lorenzo, Mauro.
Catalan: from the personal name Mauri, Latin Maurus (see Mauro ).
Catalan and French (Occitan): probably from the personal name Malric (ancient Germanic Almaricus).

variants.name: Mauro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Domenic, Giuseppe, Antonio, Carmine, Carmelo, Domenico, Gaetano, Nino, Pasquale.

Italian (mainly southern): from the personal name Mauro, derived from the Latin personal and ethnic name Maurus, borne by various early Christian saints and meaning ‘Moor, North African’. Compare Moore and Moro .

nameMeanings.name: et
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written ឯម, unexplained.
Perhaps also an altered form of German   Ehm  .

variants.name: Ehm
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto.
German: variant of Öhm (see Ohm ).

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Guillaume
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Germanic. See William .

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: Guillaume de Sainte Maure et de Précigny was born in France. He married Avoye de Sainte Maure before 1205. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died after 1209.

fr.lifeSummary: Guillaume de Sainte Maure et de Précigny est né à France. Il a épousé Avoye de Sainte Maure avant 1205. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé après 1209.

de.lifeSummary: Guillaume de Sainte Maure et de Précigny wurde in Frankreich geboren. Er hat vor 1205 Avoye de Sainte Maure geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb nach 1209.

it.lifeSummary: Guillaume de Sainte Maure et de Précigny è nato il nel Francia. Lui sposato Avoye de Sainte Maure prima del 1205. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì dopo il 1209.

ja.lifeSummary: Guillaume de Sainte Maure et de Précignyはフランスで生まれた。1205年以前にAvoye de Sainte Maureと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1209年以後に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Guillaume de Sainte Maure et de Précigny(은)는 프랑스에서 태어났다. 그는 1205년 이전 Avoye de Sainte Maure(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1209년 이후 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Guillaume de Sainte Maure et de Précigny nasceu em França. Ele casou-se com Avoye de Sainte Maure antes 1205. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu após 1209.

ru.lifeSummary: Guillaume de Sainte Maure et de Précigny родился в местности Франция. Он женился на женщине по имени Avoye de Sainte Maure перед 1205 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер после 1209 г.

es.lifeSummary: Guillaume de Sainte Maure et de Précigny nació en Francia. Se casó con Avoye de Sainte Maure antes de 1205. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió después de 1209.

th.–Female: ถึงแก่กรรม
